name: Native API (proc-self-syscall probe)
description: Execution / TA0002. Native-API techniques on Linux are largely about direct syscalls bypassing libc. As a non-malicious proxy, read /proc/self/syscall — which exposes the syscall that proc-fs uses to read syscall — and /proc/self/status. Read-only inspection of kernel-userspace boundary surfaces. No syscall is invoked outside the read itself.
exec: echo '--- /proc/self/syscall ---'; cat /proc/self/syscall 2>/dev/null | head -3 || echo '(no /proc/self/syscall)'; echo '--- /proc/self/status (first 10) ---'; head -10 /proc/self/status
